question.text: What is the minimum penalty under Section 15A for failure to furnish information, return or report to SEBI?

answer.text: Not less than one lakh rupees, extendable to one lakh per day during which failure continues, up to one crore rupees
Not less than five lakh rupees up to ten crore rupees
Not less than ten lakh rupees up to twenty-five crore rupees
Not less than fifty thousand rupees up to fifty lakh rupees

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 15A prescribes a minimum penalty of one lakh rupees, extendable to one lakh rupees for each day during which the failure continues, subject to a maximum of one crore rupees, for failure to furnish documents, maintain books, or filing false/incorrect information. **The Trap:** The higher penalties (ten lakh minimum, twenty-five crore maximum) apply to insider trading (15G) and takeover violations (15H), not to information furnishing failures. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The per-day penalty creates urgency — each day of non-compliance adds to the total penalty. Compliance officers must ensure timely filing to avoid accumulating penalties.
